seo.title: Ahrefs for Link Building for Dev Tools
seo.description: How Ahrefs fits into real Link Building for Dev Tools workflows.

content.headline: Developer Asset Linkability Scorecard
content.description: Evaluate the organic link-earning potential of a technical tool, calculator, or dataset. This analyzer benchmarks your asset against criteria known to drive citations from technical blogs, documentation, and developer communities.

inputs.name: utility_level
inputs.label: Utility Level
options: High: Solves a daily recurring task
Medium: Solves an occasional but complex task
Low: Solves a rare or niche task

inputs.name: novelty
inputs.label: Novelty Factor
options: High: No existing web-based tool exists
Medium: Better UX/UI than 2-3 existing tools

inputs.name: is_embeddable
inputs.label: Embeddable (Can others host it via iframe/script?)

inputs.name: is_open_source
inputs.label: Open Source (Is the logic public on GitHub?)

inputs.name: targets_error_codes
inputs.label: Specific Targeting (Does it solve a specific error code?)

input.utility_level: High: Solves a daily recurring task
input.novelty: Medium: Better UX/UI than 2-3 existing tools

input.utility_level: High: Solves a daily recurring task

content.how_it_works: The scorecard uses a weighted algorithm designed for the developer niche. Utility (40%) is the highest weight, as tools that solve frequent pain points are cited in internal wikis and READMEs. Novelty (30%) determines the likelihood of being featured in newsletters like TLDR or Console. Embeddability (10%) and Open Source status (10%) encourage citations from technical writers and contributors. Finally, targeting specific error codes (10%) increases visibility in search results for long-tail technical queries, leading to passive link acquisition.
